_provenance.discipline: Three categories of mandatory correction applied: (1) factual — '14-17 when sentenced; 30-35 now' → 'aged 27-36 today'; 'only a royal pardon' → multiple alternative remedies; 'he cancelled their recess' → 'told judges to stay on duty during recess'; (2) framing — 'backlog has grown 63%' → 'filings/registrations rose 63% system-wide' (the official statistic is for registrations across all Malaysian courts, not Federal Court alone, not pending backlog); 'Federal Court resumes sittings this month' → drop (unverified); 'went against rights-claimants' (both cases) → describe accurately (Heidy Quah was mixed); (3) addition — Stage 4's 'read down' detail on Section 233 narrowing prosecution scope; the 25+ juveniles detained >10 years context. Stage 5's monarchical-deference framing NOT adopted: 3R discipline + Grok itself labeled MY_OPINION on most of it.

headline: Court Filings Rose 63% in Four Years. The Chief Justice Has Named the Crisis.
context: Criminal case filings across Malaysia's courts rose from 1.5 million in 2021 to 2.5 million by November 2025 — a 63% jump. The Chief Justice has named the crisis. Two recent apex-court decisions in February and April 2026 show what is at stake.

cards.big: Criminal case filings across Malaysia's courts rose from 1.5 million in 2021 to 2.5 million by November 2025 — a 63% jump in four years.
cards.sub: The Chief Justice has named the crisis publicly. Two recent apex-court decisions show what is at stake in which cases finally get heard.
sources: Office of the Chief Registrar Federal Court of Malaysia — Opening of the Legal Year 2026 speech PDF, 12 Jan 2026, exact figures 1,522,005 → 2,486,567 (+63.37%): https://www.kehakiman.gov.my/sites/default/files/CJ%20SPEECH%20OLY%202026%20%5Bmedia%5D.pdf

SCMP, 'Malaysia's judiciary faces immense pressure as court cases surge 60%', citing the OLY 2026 speech: https://www.scmp.com/news/asia/southeast-asia/article/3339735/malaysias-judiciary-faces-immense-pressure-court-cases-surge-60-chief-justice-warns
cards.revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'backlog has grown 63%' → 'filings rose 63%' — the official statistic is for criminal case REGISTRATIONS (not pending backlog). CORRECTED (Stage 3 + Stage 4): 'Federal Court' implication dropped → 'across Malaysia's courts' (the figure is system-wide, all courts; the FC itself disposes only a few hundred matters annually). DROPPED (Stage 3): 'Federal Court resumes sittings this month' — no primary calendar confirmation; the scheduled vacation is 1-12 June 2026 per Malaysian Bar circulars.

cards.big: Heidy Quah won her free-speech case in 2025. In February 2026 the Federal Court restored the law's words — but narrowed how they can be used.
cards.sub: Section 233's words 'offensive' and 'annoy' stay in the Act. Prosecution now requires 'express intent to annoy' another user. Quah's own acquittal stood.
sources: Federal Court of Malaysia, official press summary 'Government of Malaysia v Heidy Quah Gaik Li', 6 Feb 2026 (archived PDF): https://www.article19.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/GOVERNMENT-OF-MALAYSIA-v-HEIDY-QUAH-GAIK-LI_Press_Summary.pdf
BERNAMA, 6 Feb 2026, 'Federal Court Rules Words Offensive And Annoy Under Section 233 Constitutional': https://bernama.com/en/news.php/crime_courts/news.php?id=2520759
ARTICLE 19 statement: https://www.article19.org/resources/malaysia-federal-court-decision-is-a-blow-to-freedom-of-expression/
Court of Appeal judgment 26 Aug 2025: https://www.elaw.my/JE/01/JE_2025_37.pdf
cards.revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'reinstated the law she had challenged' was OVERCLAIM — narrowed to 'restored the law's words' (specifically the impugned words 'offensive' and 'annoy' in §233(1)(a)). ADDED (Stage 4): the 'read down' detail — prosecution now requires 'express intent to annoy' — this is the substantive narrowing the apex court itself ordered. CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'went against rights-claimants' framing softened to a mixed-outcome description that accurately reflects what happened (the words returned to the Act AND her individual acquittal stood). DROPPED (Stage 3): 'used against critics for years' was UNVERIFIED — no primary enforcement records cited.

cards.big: Federal Court April 2026: seven prisoners convicted as minors lost their bid to challenge indefinite detention. The youngest is now 27.
cards.sub: Held at 'Ruler's pleasure' under Section 97(2) Child Act. The court pointed to prison-regulation reviews and the pardon process. SUHAKAM has called for reform.
sources: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026, 'Court Denies Leave To Seven Prisoners Detained At Ruler's Pleasure' — confirms current ages 27-36, applicants below 18 at time of offence: https://www.bernama.com/en/crime_courts/news.php?id=2547018
Malaysian Bar, Circular No. 150/2026, 'Nomalan Ramayah v Public Prosecutor & Other Appeals (FC)', 8 May 2026: https://www.malaysianbar.org.my/cms/upload_files/document/Circular%20No%20150-2026.pdf

SUHAKAM Office of the Children's Commissioner, Media Statement No. 21/2026, 24 Apr 2026 — calls for reform of Section 97 of the Child Act 2001: https://suhakam.org.my/2026/04/occ-media-statement-no-21-2026_childrens-commissioner-calls-for-reform-of-section-97-of-the-child-act-2001-to-ensure-legal-certainty-for-children-in-conflict-with-the-law/

FMT, 28 Apr 2026, 'Lawyers urge Parliament to address indefinite detention of child offenders': https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/04/28/lawyers-urge-parliament-to-address-indefinite-detention-of-child-offenders
cards.revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3 INCORRECT): 'aged 14-17 when sentenced; aged 30-35 now' → 'convicted as minors. The youngest is now 27' (BERNAMA-verified current ages 27-36, not 30-35; precise sentencing ages not in primary sources). CORRECTED (Stage 3 INCORRECT): 'only a royal pardon can release them' → 'the court pointed to prison-regulation reviews and the pardon process' (the FC explicitly identified multiple alternative remedies: Section 97(4) Child Act, Regulations 54 and 113 of the Prison Regulations 2000, Article 42 pardon, and annual Board of Visiting Justices recommendations). CORRECTED (Stage 3 MISLEADING): 'imprisoned since childhood' → 'convicted as minors' (the offences were committed below 18; continuous childhood imprisonment was not verified). ADDED (Stage 4): SUHAKAM reform call — primary source via OCC Media Statement No. 21/2026.

cards.big: The Chief Justice has named the crisis. He told judges to deliver judgments on time or take 'a dignified exit.' Through the June recess, registries stay open.
cards.sub: Court vacation is 'not a holiday,' he said. One Johor Bahru High Court judge handles roughly 800 cases — three times the normal load.
sources: Office of the Chief Registrar Federal Court — OLY 2026 speech PDF, 12 Jan 2026 (dignified-exit quote): https://www.kehakiman.gov.my/sites/default/files/CJ%20SPEECH%20OLY%202026%20%5Bmedia%5D.pdf
Malay Mail, 27 Apr 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/04/27/court-recess-no-time-off-chief-justice-tells-judges-to-stay-on-duty-clear-backlog/217796
The Star, 27 Apr 2026, 'CJ: Court vacation is not a holiday': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/04/27/cj-court-vacation-is-not-a-holiday
Malaysian Bar Circular No. 104/2026 confirming 1-12 June 2026 vacation period with urgent-duty arrangements: https://www.malaysianbar.org.my/cms/upload_files/document/Circular%20No%20104-2026.pdf
BERNAMA, 27 Nov 2025, on the Johor Bahru 800-cases-per-judge example: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2495995
cards.revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3 MISLEADING): 'In April he cancelled their recess' → 'Through the June recess, registries stay open' (the recess was NOT cancelled; the directive was that judges remain on duty during the 1-12 June 2026 vacation, with registries open and duty judges on call). The 'not a holiday' direct quote is preserved as verified by The Star and Malay Mail.

cards.big: Justice delayed is justice denied. But delay is not random — and someone decides whose case waits.
sources: Editorial restructure. 'Justice delayed is justice denied' is the CJ's own framing at OLY 2026 (Malaysiakini, 12 Jan 2026: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/765399). The 'someone decides whose case waits' restructure is editorial — it points at the procedural reality that priority orders, leave standards, and case-management decisions are choices, made by named actors, not natural facts.
cards.revisions: REPHRASED (Stage 5 audience check): the original 'cases that get heard before a right has aged past its purpose' was flagged as lecturer-tone. The new wording leads with the CJ's own phrase ('Justice delayed is justice denied') — a sentence Malaysians have heard — then restructures it. STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): makes the editorial point sharper without overclaiming. CORRECTED (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM): no longer implies the FC's docket is the bottleneck.

cards.big: Imagine a hospital that has nearly doubled its admissions but not its doctors. Some patients are seen quickly. Others wait through their illness.
cards.sub: The ones seen first are not always the ones whose case is most urgent. Triage is itself a choice — and someone makes it.
sources: Editorial analogy. Mirrors verified facts: criminal filings +63%, civil +60% since 2021; bench size has not expanded proportionally; leave-application and case-management mechanisms screen which cases reach the apex bench. Per OLY 2026 speech: 14 Federal Court judges, 27 Court of Appeal, 68 High Court, 38 Judicial Commissioners.
cards.revisions: Preserved unchanged. Stage 5 critiqued the analogy as 'lecturer-tone' but the structural metaphor is verifiable and accessible (hospital triage is universally understood). Stage 5's specific critique pointed at the reframe card, not the analogy.

cards.big: The Chief Justice has named the crisis and unveiled a 10-year reform plan. The harder test is whether the cases that get heard match the ones that most need to.
sources: NST, 12 Jan 2026, 'CJ unveils 10-year judicial blueprint to modernise Malaysia's courts': https://www.nst.com.my/news/crime-courts/2026/01/1358214/cj-unveils-10-year-judicial-blueprint-modernise-malaysias-courts
NST, 12 Jan 2026, 'Managing Judges appointed to oversee Sabah and Sarawak courts': https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/01/1358272/managing-judges-appointed-oversee-sabah-and-sarawak-courts

Editorial synthesis: the 'naming the crisis' part is verified (OLY 2026); the 'harder test' frames a question about which rights actually get vindicated, anchored to the Heidy Quah and seven-prisoners outcomes.
cards.revisions: ADDED (Stage 4): the 10-year Judicial Blueprint reference — Stage 4 flagged this as a structural reform context that Stage 1 missed. The reform plan (Judicial Well-Being Committee, expanded circuit sittings to Miri and Sandakan, Managing Judges for Sabah and Sarawak) is verified per NST 12 Jan 2026 coverage of the OLY 2026 announcements. STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): final card now lands with a concrete reform reference, not a hedge.

introduced_claims.claim: Criminal case filings across all Malaysian courts rose from 1.5 million in 2021 to 2.5 million by November 2025 (+63.37%).
introduced_claims.primary_source: Office of the Chief Registrar Federal Court of Malaysia — Opening of the Legal Year 2026 speech PDF, 12 Jan 2026: https://www.kehakiman.gov.my/sites/default/files/CJ%20SPEECH%20OLY%202026%20%5Bmedia%5D.pdf. Verified via Stage 3 and Stage 4. The 'across all Malaysian courts' qualifier was added in synthesis after Stage 3 flagged the system-wide-vs-Federal-Court conflation.

introduced_claims.claim: The Federal Court in February 2026 restored the words 'offensive' and 'annoy' in CMA Section 233(1)(a) but narrowed prosecution to require 'express intent to annoy.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Federal Court press summary 6 Feb 2026 (archived ARTICLE 19); BERNAMA 6 Feb 2026; ARTICLE 19 commentary identifying the 'read down' construction. Verified via Stage 4.

introduced_claims.claim: Seven prisoners convicted as minors are now aged 27-36; the Federal Court denied them leave on 20 April 2026, citing multiple alternative remedies (Section 97(4), Prison Regulations 54 and 113, Article 42 pardon, and Board of Visiting Justices reviews).
introduced_claims.primary_source: BERNAMA 20 Apr 2026; Malaysian Bar Circular No. 150/2026, 8 May 2026. Verified via Stage 3 + Stage 4. CRITICAL: corrected Stage 1's INCORRECT '14-17 when sentenced; 30-35 now' and 'only a royal pardon can release them' framings.

introduced_claims.claim: SUHAKAM's Children's Commissioner called for reform of Section 97 of the Child Act on 24 April 2026.
introduced_claims.primary_source: SUHAKAM OCC Media Statement No. 21/2026: https://suhakam.org.my/2026/04/occ-media-statement-no-21-2026_childrens-commissioner-calls-for-reform-of-section-97-of-the-child-act-2001-to-ensure-legal-certainty-for-children-in-conflict-with-the-law/. Verified via Stage 4 + Stage 5.

introduced_claims.claim: The CJ unveiled a 10-year Judicial Blueprint at OLY 2026, including Managing Judges for Sabah and Sarawak, Judicial Well-Being Committee, and expanded circuit sittings.
introduced_claims.primary_source: NST 12 Jan 2026 (two articles): https://www.nst.com.my/news/crime-courts/2026/01/1358214/cj-unveils-10-year-judicial-blueprint-modernise-malaysias-courts and https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/01/1358272/managing-judges-appointed-oversee-sabah-and-sarawak-courts. Verified via Stage 4.

introduced_claims.claim: The June 2026 court vacation runs 1-12 June 2026 with registries open and urgent matters handled by designated duty judges.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Malaysian Bar Circular No. 104/2026 (26 March 2026) and Circular No. 131/2026 (21 April 2026); Malay Mail 27 Apr 2026. Verified via Stage 3.

synthesis_notes:
Four over-integration decisions:

1. **Hook framing** — Stage 3 said 'backlog has grown 63%' was OVERCLAIM (the stat is filings/registrations, not pending backlog) AND MISLEADING_FRAMING (the stat is system-wide, not Federal Court). Stage 5 wanted sharper language. The right answer is Stage 3's correction: rephrase to 'criminal case filings across Malaysia's courts rose 63%.' We do not adopt Stage 5's harder framing about 'apex court at the top of an overloaded system' because that framing itself implies a structural conflation Stage 3 already flagged. The corrected wording is more accurate AND less editorialized — Stage 3 wins here.

2. **Heidy Quah card** — Stage 3 said 'went against rights-claimant' was MISLEADING (mixed outcome). Stage 5 said the analysis was 'cowardly' for not naming the free-speech reversal. The right answer is to describe accurately what happened: the law's words were restored, the prosecution scope narrowed by 'reading down,' and Quah's individual acquittal stood. This is more informative than either pure-loss or pure-win framing. Stage 4's 'read down' detail is the synthesis value-add.

3. **Seven prisoners card** — Stage 3 flagged two INCORRECT claims (ages, sole-remedy framing). Stage 5 wanted to escalate to 'monarchical deference' framing. The right answer is to apply Stage 3's corrections completely (BERNAMA-verified ages 27-36; multiple remedy paths) AND to NOT adopt Stage 5's monarchical-deference framing (Stage 5 itself self-labeled MY_OPINION on most of the connected claims; 3R discipline applies). Adding the SUHAKAM reform call gives the card a sourced civil-society response that doesn't editorialize about royal prerogative.

4. **'In April he cancelled their recess'** — Stage 3 said MISLEADING because the recess wasn't actually cancelled. The CJ told judges to remain on duty during the scheduled 1-12 June 2026 vacation. Stage 5 said this should be framed harder ('the political class has done nothing'). The right answer is Stage 3's factual correction: 'Through the June recess, registries stay open.' Stage 5's harder framing requires going beyond what the primary sources support.

Compression decisions: Dropped 'used against critics for years' (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED). Dropped the May 2026 Federal Court sitting-restart claim entirely from the hook (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED). The view card was rewritten to incorporate the 10-year Judicial Blueprint (Stage 4) rather than land on a pure hedge. The reframe was rewritten to use the CJ's own 'justice delayed is justice denied' phrase as the anchor (Stage 5's audience-accessibility critique).

What was NOT adopted: Stage 5's monarchical-deference framing (3R + self-labeled MY_OPINION); Stage 5's 'bipartisan political neglect' framing (verified by SCMP but partisan-coded for T4A); Stage 4's international parallels (good context but doesn't fit the 7-card budget); Stage 4's Syariah-court / gender extension (real but out of scope for this issue's editorial frame).
confidence: High on: criminal-filings growth (CJ OLY 2026 speech PDF — primary), Heidy Quah Feb 2026 partial reversal + 'read down' construction (FC press summary archived by ARTICLE 19 + BERNAMA + Skrine legal analysis), seven prisoners' April 2026 leave-denial (BERNAMA + Malaysian Bar Circular 150/2026 + SUHAKAM Media Statement), CJ's 'dignified exit' and 'not a holiday' direct quotes (multi-outlet primary), 800-cases Johor Bahru example (BERNAMA 27 Nov 2025), 1-12 June 2026 vacation dates (Malaysian Bar circulars), 10-year Judicial Blueprint and Managing Judges (NST 12 Jan 2026). Medium on: the specific bench composition for the Heidy Quah judgment (the FC press summary states the full judgment is the only authoritative document; the press summary itself is what we have). Medium on: exact ages at time of sentencing for the seven prisoners (BERNAMA verifies current ages 27-36 but exact sentencing ages were not pulled to primary records). Medium on the editorial 'someone decides whose case waits' restructure — that is value-restructuring across the verified facts, flagged in the reframe.

scoring.sr_rationale: Synthesis applies every single Stage 3 cardinal-sin correction (7 of them) and integrates Stage 4's most important addition (the 'read down' construction on Section 233; the multi-remedy reality of the seven-prisoners ruling; the SUHAKAM reform call; the 10-year Judicial Blueprint). The headline and hook are now factually precise about what the 63% statistic measures (filings, system-wide) — eliminating the most serious overclaim Stage 1 made. The seven-prisoners card now reflects what the Federal Court actually said. Capped at 86 (not higher) because: (a) Stage 5's audience-accessibility critique (lecturer-tone) is only partially addressed — the reframe was rewritten but the hospital analogy and view-card register are still somewhat institutional; (b) we deliberately did not adopt Stage 5's monarchical-deference framing despite it being verified via SUHAKAM — that's a 3R-discipline choice we document but it does leave the seven-prisoners card less aggressive than Stage 5 wanted; (c) several Stage 4 dimensions (gender, class, East Malaysia) were not incorporated due to the 7-card budget — noted in synthesis_notes.

_self_check.overclaim_check: All 7 Stage 3 cardinal-sin flags applied. The 63% is now framed as filings (not backlog) and system-wide (not Federal Court). The seven prisoners' current ages (27-36) match BERNAMA. The court's multi-remedy framing replaces the single-pardon overclaim. The recess directive is described as 'registries stay open' not 'cancelled.' Heidy Quah is described as mixed-outcome. 'Used against critics for years' removed.
_self_check.underclaim_check: Where Stage 3 verifications are HIGH (the 63.37% growth, the FC's restoration of the impugned words, the seven prisoners' April 2026 leave-denial, the CJ's direct quotes), we state them clearly. The 'someone decides whose case waits' restructure is firm — not hedged into 'might be argued.'
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: No selective dates. The 63% statistic is correctly attributed (criminal filings, 2021-Nov 2025, all Malaysian courts). The Heidy Quah outcome is described accurately (mixed). The seven-prisoners ruling is described as a leave-denial citing multiple alternative remedies, not a substantive constitutional ruling. The CJ directives are sequenced chronologically (January OLY → April recess directive) with accurate factual descriptions.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific traces to a primary or near-primary source. The 'read down' construction is sourced to ARTICLE 19 + FC press summary. The 27-36 age range is sourced to BERNAMA. The Section 97(4) / Prison Regulations 54 and 113 / Article 42 / Board of Visiting Justices remedies are sourced to BERNAMA + Malaysian Bar Circular 150/2026. The 10-year Blueprint and Managing Judges are sourced to NST 12 Jan 2026. SUHAKAM reform call sourced to OCC Media Statement No. 21/2026.
_self_check.3R_special_caution: Section 97(2) Child Act detention 'at Ruler's pleasure' is named per the statute. We deliberately do NOT adopt Stage 5's 'monarchical deference' framing despite it being defensible — Stage 5 itself self-labeled MY_OPINION on several connected claims, and 3R discipline counsels reporting the legal mechanism without speculating on royal preferences or motivations. The view card and reframe carry no royal characterisation. Card 3 sub says 'the pardon process' as one of multiple remedies — accurate to the FC's own framing.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All cards target institutions (Federal Court, Chief Justice as office-holder, Court of Appeal, the Act, SUHAKAM) and the structural pattern. The CJ Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh is named in source attribution but not characterised in card bodies. Heidy Quah and the seven prisoners are named because they are the named parties in public court records.
_self_check.anger_anxiety_check: Dominant emotion target: structural anxiety about which rights get vindicated when. We deliberately step back from Stage 5's anger-at-power escalation (monarchical deference, bipartisan neglect) and stay with the verified facts: the system is overwhelmed; the CJ has named it; two recent apex-court decisions illustrate what is at stake; reforms have been announced. The reader's energy is directed toward the structural question of triage, not at any individual or institution.
